perturbation.original_text: Contractor agrees that all right, title and interest in any and all copyrights, copyright registrations and copyrightable subject matter which occur as a result of our work for the Company shall be the sole and exclusive property of the Company, and agree that such works comprise works made for hire. Contractor hereby assigns, and agree to assign, all right, title and interest in any and all copyrights, copyright registration and copyrightable subject matter which occur as a result of our work for the Company.
perturbation.changed_text: Contractor agrees that all right, title and interest in any and all copyrights, copyright registrations and copyrightable subject matter which occur as a result of our work for the Company shall be the sole and exclusive property of the Company, *except for works that are predominantly based on Contractor's prior work*, and agree that such works comprise works made for hire. Contractor hereby assigns, and agree to assign, all right, title and interest in any and all copyrights, copyright registration and copyrightable subject matter which occur as a result of our work for the Company.
perturbation.explanation: Adding 'except for works that are predominantly based on Contractor's prior work' creates an exception to the copyright assignment. The original text says all copyrights belong to the Company, while the new language allows the contractor to retain rights to certain works. This exception introduces uncertainty about ownership of works, because it is not clearly defined, when the contractors work is considered predominately based on 'prior work' impacting enforcement and creating an in text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Contractor agrees that all right, title and interest in any and all copyrights, copyright registrations and copyrightable subject matter which occur as a result of our work for the Company shall be the sole and exclusive property of the Company, *except for works that are predominantly based on Contractor's prior work*, and agree that such works comprise works made for hire. Contractor hereby assigns, and agree to assign, all right, title and interest in any and all copyrights, copyright registration and copyrightable subject matter which occur as a result of our work for the Company.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces an exception ('except for works that are predominantly based on Contractor's prior work') to the copyright assignment. The original text states that all copyrights belong to the Company. The added exception creates uncertainty about ownership of certain works and contradicts the original statement, creating a strong in-text contradiction. The Contradicted Text and Changed text are also located in the same area which creates an in text contradiction.
